note: Project-scoped. Loads only when the BriefSpec project-type is a checkout/commerce flow. Highest-leverage, empirically-tested checkout standards (Baymard large-N). Most are detectable in generated HTML, so they map to the deterministic gate; the rest route to the judge.

standards.rule: Request only strictly-needed fields; never ask for cardholder name if it is derivable or unnecessary.
Card-field order is number, then expiry, then CVC, with name last or removed; never name-first.
Every field carries the correct HTML autocomplete token (cc-number, cc-exp, postal-code, email) and input type/inputmode for a numeric keypad.
Offer guest checkout; never force account creation before purchase.
Validate fields inline in real time, with specific error text placed adjacent to the field.
Persist and pre-fill values the user already entered earlier in the flow (cross-cutting WCAG 3.3.7 Redundant Entry).
standards.source_title: WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.7 Redundant Entry (applied to checkout)
